question_text: Define static electricity and give one example of where it occurs naturally.
correct_answer: Static electricity is the buildup of electric charge on the surface of objects, usually caused by friction. An example is lightning during a thunderstorm, which occurs when charge builds up in clouds.
explanation: Static electricity is different from current electricity because the charge stays in one place rather than flowing. Lightning is a natural example of static electricity where charge builds up in storm clouds and suddenly discharges to the ground with enormous energy.
